articles.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' Economic Roadmap for Developed Nation Status
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the ambitious 'Vision Bharat 2047' economic roadmap, outlining a comprehensive strategy to transform India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The roadmap focuses on five pillars: sustainable growth, technological innovation, inclusive development, global economic integration, and robust governance.
• Key initiatives include massive infrastructure investment, skill development programs for the future workforce, promotion of green technologies, and reforms to boost manufacturing and exports.
• A dedicated task force, comprising experts from NITI Aayog, various ministries, and private sector leaders, will oversee the implementation and progress of the vision.
• The plan aims to significantly increase per capita income, reduce poverty, and improve India's ranking on global human development indices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Vision Bharat 2047 aims for developed nation status by 2047.
Five pillars: sustainable growth, technological innovation, inclusive development, global economic integration, robust governance.
Implementation overseen by a task force with NITI Aayog involvement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a long-term economic roadmap like Vision Bharat 2047 for India's developmental trajectory.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving the 'developed nation' goal by 2047, considering the five identified pillars.
Evaluate the potential impact of proposed reforms on various sectors of the Indian economy and society.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic representing India's economic growth trajectory towards 2047.
articles.trivia: India's first five-year plan was launched in 1951, focusing on agricultural development.

articles.headline: Sculptor Ram V. Sutar Celebrates 100th Birthday, Legacy of Iconic Statues
articles.explainer:
• Renowned Indian sculptor Ram V. Sutar, the creative force behind the Statue of Unity, celebrates his 100th birthday today, marking a century of artistic excellence and contributions to public art.
• Sutar is best known for designing the Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue, depicting Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, which stands 182 meters tall in Gujarat.
• His extensive body of work includes hundreds of statues of national leaders, freedom fighters, and cultural icons, installed across India and abroad.
• A recipient of the Padma Bhushan and Padma Shri, Sutar's artistic style is characterized by a blend of realism and monumentality, capturing the essence of his subjects.
• His contributions have significantly shaped India's public spaces and national iconography, inspiring a sense of pride and historical remembrance.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ram V. Sutar is the sculptor of the Statue of Unity.
The Statue of Unity depicts Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.
He is a recipient of Padma Bhushan and Padma Shri.
His 100th birthday is celebrated on December 18, 2025.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of monumental public art in shaping national identity and historical consciousness in India, with examples like the Statue of Unity.
Analyze the artistic style and contributions of Ram V. Sutar to modern Indian sculpture.
Evaluate the cultural and socio-political significance of commissioning large-scale statues of national leaders.
articles.imageDescription: Image of Ram V. Sutar alongside a miniature model of the Statue of Unity.
articles.trivia: The Statue of Unity is located on an island in the Narmada River, facing the Sardar Sarovar Dam.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Affirmative Action Framework in Promotions
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of the existing framework for affirmative action in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs) in public employment.
• The court emphasized that the state has a compelling interest in ensuring adequate representation for marginalized communities, provided the criteria for 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation' are regularly reviewed.
• The ruling clarified the parameters for data collection required to justify such reservations, mandating quantifiable data on backwardness and representation across various cadres.
• This decision brings clarity to a long-standing legal debate, aiming to balance the principles of equality and merit while addressing historical injustices.
• It directs the Union and State governments to finalize and implement robust mechanisms for periodic review and data collection within six months.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld affirmative action in promotions for SC/STs.
Emphasized regular review of 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation'.
Mandated quantifiable data collection for justification.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional principles and socio-economic justifications behind affirmative action in public employment in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing reservations in promotions, particularly concerning the collection of quantifiable data and the balance with merit.
Evaluate the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on administrative efficiency and social justice for marginalized communities.
articles.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
articles.trivia: Article 16(4A) and 16(4B) of the Indian Constitution deal with reservations in promotions and carrying forward unfilled vacancies.

articles.headline: India and Japan Conclude High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Energy
articles.explainer:
• India and Japan successfully concluded their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in Indo-Pacific security, defense manufacturing, and green energy transition.
• The two nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing on joint maritime exercises and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• A new framework for technology transfer and co-production in defense was announced, aiming to bolster India's 'Make in India' initiative and Japan's defense capabilities.
• Significant agreements were signed to accelerate investment in India's green hydrogen and renewable energy sectors, aligning with global climate goals.
• Both sides also discussed expanding collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, including AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security and green energy.
Agreements on defense technology transfer and co-production.
Collaboration in green hydrogen, renewable energy, AI, quantum computing, semiconductors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of Indo-Pacific geopolitical dynamics.
Discuss the potential of India-Japan cooperation in defense manufacturing and green energy transition for both nations' strategic autonomy and economic growth.
Evaluate how this partnership contributes to regional stability and addresses challenges posed by emerging technologies.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: India and Japan established diplomatic relations in 1952.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, marking a major step towards cost-effective space access.
• The test involved a scaled-down prototype demonstrating autonomous landing capabilities under simulated operational conditions, validating key aerodynamic and guidance systems.
• This achievement is crucial for ISRO's long-term goal of developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, significantly reducing the cost per launch.
• The RLV program aims to enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market, allowing for frequent and affordable access to space for satellites and future human spaceflight missions.
• Further tests are planned, including orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight demonstrations, as ISRO pushes the boundaries of space technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: ISRO successfully tested Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology.
Test involved autonomous landing capabilities of a scaled-down prototype.
RLV aims to reduce launch costs and enhance India's space competitiveness.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of Reusable Launch Vehicle technology for India's space program and its implications for the global space industry.
Analyze the technological challenges and economic benefits associated with developing fully reusable launch systems.
Evaluate how ISRO's RLV program aligns with India's broader strategic goals in space exploration and commercial space ventures.
articles.imageDescription: Conceptual image of ISRO's Reusable Launch Vehicle prototype landing on a runway.
articles.trivia: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is currently one of the most successful operational reusable orbital-class rockets.

articles.headline: National Green Hydrogen Mission Targets Revised Upwards Amidst Global Demand Surge
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet today approved an upward revision of targets under the National Green Hydrogen Mission, aiming to significantly boost India's production capacity by 2030.
• The new targets now aim for 7 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production annually, up from the initial 5 MMT, driven by rising global demand and technological advancements.
• The revised mission includes enhanced incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers and producing green hydrogen, alongside developing dedicated green hydrogen hubs.
• This strategic move positions India as a potential global leader in green hydrogen export and helps decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers.
• The government also announced plans for robust infrastructure development, including pipelines and storage facilities, to support the expanded production and distribution network.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: National Green Hydrogen Mission targets revised to 7 MMT annual production by 2030.
Includes enhanced incentives for electrolyzer manufacturing and green hydrogen production.
Aims to decarbonize hard-to-abate sectors and position India as a global exporter.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy transition, climate goals, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and developing its infrastructure in India.
Evaluate the potential impact of green hydrogen on various industrial sectors and India's position in the global energy market.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic showing the process of green hydrogen production using renewable energy.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe.

articles.headline: National Digital Health Mission Launches Unified Health Information Platform for Citizens
articles.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) today launched its Unified Health Information Platform (UHIP), integrating various digital health services and records for citizens across the country.
• UHIP enables individuals to create unique health IDs, access their medical records securely, and connect with healthcare providers and facilities through a single portal.
• The platform aims to streamline healthcare delivery, improve patient care coordination, and facilitate data-driven policy-making in the public health sector.
• Key features include telemedicine integration, e-pharmacy services, and a consent-based framework for sharing health data with authorized entities.
• This initiative is a significant step towards achieving universal health coverage and building a resilient, accessible, and efficient digital health ecosystem in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: NDHM launched Unified Health Information Platform (UHIP).
UHIP integrates digital health services and records.
Enables unique health IDs, secure medical record access, telemedicine, and e-pharmacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of digital health platforms like UHIP in improving healthcare accessibility and efficiency in India.
Analyze the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy in the widespread adoption of such platforms.
Evaluate how the National Digital Health Mission contributes to achieving the goals of universal health coverage and a resilient health system.
articles.imageDescription: Illustration of a person accessing digital health records on a mobile device.
articles.trivia: India's Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is the foundational framework for the NDHM.

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Repo Rate at 6.5% in Latest Monetary Policy Review Amidst Stable Inflation Outlook
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) today announced its decision to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5% during its latest Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) review.
• The decision was unanimous, citing a stable inflation outlook within the target range and the need to support economic growth amidst global uncertainties.
• The MPC also maintained its 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance, indicating a cautious approach to liquidity management.
• RBI projected India's GDP growth at 7.0% for the current fiscal year, while retail inflation is expected to average around 4.5%.
• Governor Shaktikanta Das highlighted global geopolitical risks and commodity price volatility as key factors influencing future policy decisions.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
Government Budgeting.
Investment models.
prelimsPoints: RBI kept repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decision was unanimous.
Inflation outlook stable, GDP growth projected at 7.0% for current fiscal.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of the RBI's Monetary Policy Committee in maintaining price stability and supporting economic growth in India.
Analyze the factors that influence the MPC's decision-making, including inflation trends, global economic conditions, and domestic growth prospects.
Evaluate the implications of the 'withdrawal of accommodation' stance on liquidity and credit availability in the Indian economy.
articles.imageDescription: Building of the Reserve Bank of India in Mumbai.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India has six members: three from the RBI and three external members appointed by the government.

articles.headline: Centre Launches 'Amrit Dharohar 2.0' for Urban Flood Resilience and Wetland Conservation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change today launched 'Amrit Dharohar 2.0', an integrated initiative focusing on urban flood resilience and enhanced wetland conservation across 50 major Indian cities.
• The program aims to develop climate-resilient urban infrastructure, including improved drainage systems, permeable surfaces, and green spaces to mitigate flood risks.
• A crucial component involves the restoration and conservation of urban wetlands, recognizing their role as natural sponges and biodiversity hotspots.
• The initiative promotes community participation and leverages satellite imagery and AI for real-time flood forecasting and wetland monitoring.
• 'Amrit Dharohar 2.0' aligns with India's commitments under the Ramsar Convention and urban sustainability goals.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Disaster and disaster management.
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
articles.source: Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change
prelimsPoints: Amrit Dharohar 2.0 launched for urban flood resilience and wetland conservation.
Covers 50 major Indian cities.
Focuses on climate-resilient infrastructure and restoration of urban wetlands.
Leverages satellite imagery and AI for flood forecasting.
mainsPoints: Discuss the increasing vulnerability of Indian cities to urban flooding and the role of infrastructure and natural ecosystems in mitigating these risks.
Analyze how initiatives like 'Amrit Dharohar 2.0' integrate disaster management, urban planning, and environmental conservation goals.
Evaluate the significance of wetland conservation for urban biodiversity, water management, and climate resilience.
articles.imageDescription: Satellite image showing an urban area with green spaces and water bodies, highlighting flood mitigation.
articles.trivia: India is a signatory to the Ramsar Convention on Wetlands, an international treaty for the conservation and sustainable use of wetlands.

articles.headline: Parliament Passes E-Waste (Management and Handling) Bill, 2025
articles.explainer:
• The Parliament today passed the E-Waste (Management and Handling) Bill, 2025, introducing stricter regulations for the collection, recycling, and disposal of electronic waste.
• The new legislation mandates extended producer responsibility (EPR) for manufacturers, requiring them to collect specific percentages of their products at the end of their life cycle.
• It also introduces provisions for formalizing the informal e-waste recycling sector, ensuring worker safety and environmentally sound practices.
• The Bill aims to curb illegal e-waste dumping, promote circular economy principles, and reduce the environmental and health hazards associated with improper e-waste management.
• Penalties for non-compliance have been significantly increased, and a national e-waste tracking system will be established for greater transparency.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
prelimsPoints: E-Waste (Management and Handling) Bill, 2025 passed by Parliament.
Introduces stricter regulations for e-waste collection, recycling, disposal.
Mandates Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for manufacturers.
Aims to formalize informal sector and establish a national tracking system.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of e-waste management in India and its environmental and health implications.
Analyze the key provisions of the E-Waste (Management and Handling) Bill, 2025, particularly Extended Producer Responsibility and formalization of the informal sector.
Evaluate how this legislation promotes circular economy principles and contributes to sustainable resource management in India.
articles.imageDescription: Pile of electronic waste components awaiting recycling.
articles.trivia: India is among the top five e-waste generating countries globally.

articles.headline: India Initiates Further Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions with Pakistan
articles.explainer:
• India has initiated a further review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's interpretation and implementation of the treaty's clauses.
• The review focuses on aspects related to water usage for run-of-the-river hydropower projects on the Western rivers allocated to Pakistan, which India asserts are within its rights.
• This development follows ongoing disagreements and calls for a more equitable sharing of waters, particularly concerning projects like Kishanganga and Ratle.
• India has consistently emphasized its commitment to the IWT but also its right to utilize its share of waters for domestic, agricultural, and power generation purposes.
• Diplomatic channels remain open, but India's move signals a firmer stance on protecting its water interests in the region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India initiated further review of Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) provisions with Pakistan.
Concerns over Pakistan's interpretation of water usage for run-of-the-river hydropower projects on Western rivers.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the complexities and challenges in implementing the IWT, particularly concerning India's right to utilize Western river waters for hydropower.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's review of the treaty and potential pathways for resolving water disputes between the two nations.
articles.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers under the IWT.
articles.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Endorse New Framework for Climate Finance Mobilization
articles.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors, meeting in Riyadh, today endorsed a new framework aimed at accelerating the mobilization of climate finance for developing nations.
• The framework emphasizes innovative financial instruments, blended finance solutions, and increased participation from multilateral development banks and the private sector.
• It also calls for greater transparency in climate finance tracking and reporting, along with capacity building for developing countries to access and utilize funds effectively.
• India played a crucial role in advocating for 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate finance discussions.
• The agreement is expected to pave the way for more robust financial flows towards climate adaptation, mitigation, and resilience projects globally.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
keyTerms: CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities)
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers endorsed a new framework for climate finance mobilization.
Emphasizes innovative financial instruments and blended finance.
India advocated for CBDR-RC principles.
Aims to increase financial flows for climate adaptation and mitigation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of climate finance in enabling developing nations to achieve their climate goals and the challenges in its mobilization.
Analyze the key features of the new G20 framework for climate finance and its potential impact on global climate action.
Evaluate India's position and contributions to international climate finance discussions, particularly concerning equity and historical responsibility.
articles.imageDescription: G20 logo prominently displayed at a conference venue.
articles.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

articles.headline: Government Introduces New Framework for Minimum Support Price (MSP) Determination and Procurement
articles.explainer:
• The Union Agriculture Ministry today unveiled a new, comprehensive framework for the determination and procurement of Minimum Support Price (MSP) for 23 mandated crops, aiming for greater transparency and farmer income stability.
• The framework incorporates recommendations from various expert committees, focusing on a formula that accounts for comprehensive cost of production (C2+50%) and market demand-supply dynamics.
• It introduces a tiered procurement system, combining direct government purchase with a price deficiency payment scheme for certain crops, to ensure broader coverage and reduce market distortions.
• The initiative also includes provisions for real-time monitoring of market prices and procurement operations through a digital platform, enhancing efficiency and accountability.
• This reform package seeks to address long-standing farmer grievances, reduce post-harvest losses, and incentivize crop diversification towards high-value crops.
gsSubTopics: Major crops-cropping patterns in various parts of the country, - different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
Food processing and related industries in India- scope’ and significance, location, upstream and downstream requirements, supply chain management.
prelimsPoints: New MSP framework for 23 mandated crops introduced.
Formula accounts for comprehensive cost of production (C2+50%) and market dynamics.
Includes a tiered procurement system and price deficiency payments.
Digital platform for real-time monitoring of market prices and procurement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolution of Minimum Support Price (MSP) policy in India and its impact on agricultural economics and farmer welfare.
Analyze the key features of the new MSP framework, particularly the C2+50% formula and tiered procurement system, and their potential benefits and challenges.
Evaluate how these reforms aim to address farmer grievances, ensure income stability, and promote sustainable agricultural practices in India.
articles.imageDescription: Farmers harvesting crops in a field, with a tractor in the background.
articles.trivia: The Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP) recommends MSPs for 22 mandated crops and fair and remunerative price (FRP) for sugarcane.

articles.headline: UNESCO Designates Two New Indian Sites as World Heritage, Boosting Cultural Tourism
articles.explainer:
• UNESCO today announced the inclusion of two significant Indian sites – the 'Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura' and the 'Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh' – in its prestigious World Heritage List.
• The Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura, known for their intricate sculptures and Buddhist heritage, represent a unique blend of art and spiritual traditions in Northeast India.
• The Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh, a series of strategic fortifications along major rivers, exemplify medieval Indian military architecture and urban planning.
• These designations are expected to significantly boost cultural tourism to these regions, promoting local economies and encouraging conservation efforts.
• India now boasts a total of 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, solidifying its position as a global cultural heritage destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Two new Indian sites added to UNESCO World Heritage List: Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura and Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh.
India now has 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
These sites represent Buddhist heritage, art, and medieval military architecture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for the conservation, promotion, and economic development of cultural sites in India.
Analyze the unique historical and architectural importance of the 'Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura' and the 'Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh'.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in managing and preserving such diverse heritage sites while promoting sustainable tourism.
articles.imageDescription: Composite image showing a rock-cut cave entrance and a historic riverine fort.
articles.trivia: The first Indian sites to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1983 were the Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, Agra Fort, and the Taj Mahal.

articles.headline: National Cybersecurity Agency Proposes Enhanced Framework for Critical Infrastructure Protection
articles.explainer:
• The National Cybersecurity Agency (NCA) today released a draft of its enhanced cybersecurity framework specifically designed for critical infrastructure sectors, including energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• The framework mandates stricter compliance standards, regular audits, and proactive threat intelligence sharing among public and private entities operating critical infrastructure.
• It emphasizes a 'zero-trust' architecture, robust incident response protocols, and continuous employee training to counter evolving cyber threats from state and non-state actors.
• The proposal also includes provisions for establishing a dedicated 'Cyber Crisis Response Team' for each critical sector, capable of rapid deployment during major cyber incidents.
• This move aims to bolster India's cyber resilience and protect essential services from sophisticated cyberattacks, aligning with global best practices.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention.
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism.
articles.source: Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology
prelimsPoints: NCA released draft enhanced cybersecurity framework for critical infrastructure.
Framework mandates stricter compliance, regular audits, and threat intelligence sharing.
Emphasizes 'zero-trust' architecture and dedicated 'Cyber Crisis Response Team' per sector.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threats to India's critical infrastructure from cyberattacks and their potential impact on national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key components of the proposed enhanced cybersecurity framework, including 'zero-trust' architecture and incident response protocols, and their effectiveness.
Evaluate the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework across diverse public and private critical infrastructure sectors in India.
articles.imageDescription: Abstract digital image representing cybersecurity with network lines and lock icons.
articles.trivia: India's National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) is the nodal agency for protecting critical information infrastructure.

articles.headline: NITI Aayog Releases Comprehensive Guidelines for Ethical Artificial Intelligence Development in India
articles.explainer:
• NITI Aayog today released its much-anticipated comprehensive guidelines for the ethical development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in India, emphasizing principles of fairness, transparency, and accountability.
• The guidelines provide a framework for both public and private sector entities to ensure responsible AI innovation, addressing concerns related to data privacy, bias, and human oversight.
• Key recommendations include independent auditing of AI systems, impact assessments for high-risk applications, and mechanisms for redressal against AI-driven decisions.
• This move positions India as a leader in promoting responsible AI governance globally, balancing technological advancement with societal well-being.
• NITI Aayog will establish a dedicated 'AI Ethics Council' to monitor adherence to these guidelines and update them as AI technology evolves.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Awareness in the fields of Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: NITI Aayog released guidelines for ethical AI development in India.
Focuses on fairness, transparency, accountability, data privacy, bias, human oversight.
Recommends independent auditing, impact assessments, and redressal mechanisms.
Dedicated 'AI Ethics Council' to be established.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ethical challenges and societal implications arising from the rapid development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence.
Analyze the key principles and recommendations of NITI Aayog's guidelines for ethical AI and their role in fostering responsible innovation in India.
Evaluate the effectiveness of regulatory frameworks in balancing technological progress with human values and addressing concerns like bias and accountability in AI systems.
articles.imageDescription: Stylized graphic of a human hand interacting with a robotic hand, symbolizing AI ethics.
articles.trivia: NITI Aayog released a National Strategy for Artificial Intelligence in 2018, titled 'AI for All'.

articles.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches 'Setu-Bharat' Cultural Exchange Program with Southeast Asian Nations
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture today inaugurated 'Setu-Bharat', a new cultural exchange program aimed at strengthening ancient civilizational ties and contemporary artistic collaborations with Southeast Asian nations.
• The initiative will facilitate exchanges of artists, scholars, and cultural practitioners, promoting shared heritage in performing arts, literature, and traditional crafts.
• Key activities include joint archaeological research, heritage conservation projects, and festivals showcasing the diverse cultures of India and ASEAN member states.
• 'Setu-Bharat' seeks to enhance India's soft power diplomacy and foster greater understanding and goodwill in the region.
• The program aligns with India's 'Act East Policy' and emphasizes the historical maritime and cultural links that bind India with Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture launched 'Setu-Bharat' cultural exchange program with Southeast Asian nations.
Aims to strengthen civilizational ties and artistic collaborations.
Includes joint archaeological research, heritage conservation, and cultural festivals.
Aligns with India's 'Act East Policy'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of cultural diplomacy and soft power in strengthening India's bilateral and regional relations, particularly with Southeast Asian nations.
Analyze how programs like 'Setu-Bharat' leverage shared historical and cultural heritage to foster greater understanding and cooperation.
Evaluate the significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in promoting cultural and economic engagement with the ASEAN region.
articles.imageDescription: Collage of traditional Indian and Southeast Asian cultural symbols and art forms.
articles.trivia: The term 'Act East Policy' succeeded the 'Look East Policy' in 2014, with a greater focus on strategic and economic engagement.

articles.headline: Finance Commission Recommends Enhanced Gender Budgeting Framework for State Governments
articles.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission, in its interim report submitted today, recommended an enhanced framework for gender budgeting to be adopted by all state governments across India.
• The recommendations include mandatory allocation of a specific percentage of state budgets towards schemes directly benefiting women and girls, with robust monitoring mechanisms.
• The framework emphasizes disaggregated data collection on gender-sensitive indicators to assess the impact and effectiveness of public expenditure.
• It also suggests capacity building for state finance departments and local bodies to integrate a gender perspective into planning and resource allocation.
• This move aims to promote gender equality, empower women, and ensure that public spending effectively addresses gender disparities in development outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: 16th Finance Commission recommended enhanced gender budgeting framework for state governments.
Mandates specific budget allocation for schemes benefiting women and girls.
Emphasizes disaggregated data collection and capacity building.
Aims to promote gender equality and address disparities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept and significance of gender budgeting in promoting gender equality and inclusive development in India.
Analyze the recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission for state governments regarding gender budgeting and their potential impact on public finance and women's empowerment.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of gender budgeting, including data availability, political will, and capacity constraints at the state level.
articles.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the concept of gender equality with balanced scales.
articles.trivia: India introduced gender budgeting statements in its Union Budget for the first time in 2005-06.

articles.headline: Scientists Discover Three New Endemic Species in Biodiversity Hotspot of Western Ghats
articles.explainer:
• A team of Indian scientists today announced the discovery of three new endemic species – a vibrant orchid, a unique freshwater fish, and a new frog species – in the biodiverse Western Ghats region.
• The orchid species (Orchidaceae family) was found in the evergreen forests of Kerala, showcasing distinct floral patterns.
• The freshwater fish (Cyprinidae family) was identified in a remote stream in Karnataka, exhibiting unique adaptations to its habitat.
• The new frog species (Dicroglossidae family) was discovered in the high-altitude shola grasslands of Tamil Nadu, adding to the amphibian diversity of the region.
• These discoveries underscore the immense biodiversity wealth of the Western Ghats, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot, emphasizing the urgent need for its conservation.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity and its conservation.
prelimsPoints: Three new endemic species (orchid, freshwater fish, frog) discovered in Western Ghats.
Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a global biodiversity hotspot.
Discoveries were made in Kerala, Karnataka, and Tamil Nadu.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for scientific research, conservation planning, and understanding ecosystem health.
Evaluate the strategies required for effective biodiversity conservation and sustainable management of ecologically sensitive areas like the Western Ghats.
articles.imageDescription: Composite image showing a colorful orchid, a freshwater fish, and a frog in their natural habitats.
articles.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountains.

articles.headline: AYUSH Ministry Launches Advanced Yoga Protocol for Mental Well-being and Stress Management
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH today unveiled an advanced yoga protocol specifically designed to enhance mental well-being, manage stress, and mitigate anxiety in daily life.
• Developed in collaboration with leading yoga institutions and mental health experts, the protocol integrates specific asanas, pranayama, meditation techniques, and mindfulness practices.
• The initiative targets a broad demographic, including students, working professionals, and the elderly, providing accessible tools for mental health support.
• Training modules and digital resources will be made available to promote widespread adoption and ensure correct practice of the protocol.
• This launch underscores the government's commitment to promoting holistic health and leveraging traditional Indian practices for contemporary health challenges.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: AYUSH Ministry launched an advanced yoga protocol for mental well-being and stress management.
Integrates asanas, pranayama, meditation, and mindfulness.
Targets students, professionals, and elderly.
Promotes holistic health using traditional Indian practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of mental health issues in contemporary society and the role of traditional practices like yoga in addressing them.
Analyze the components and potential benefits of the AYUSH Ministry's advanced yoga protocol for mental well-being and stress management.
Evaluate the government's efforts in promoting holistic health and integrating traditional medicine systems into mainstream healthcare for public welfare.
articles.imageDescription: People performing yoga postures in a serene outdoor setting.
articles.trivia: The International Day of Yoga is celebrated annually on June 21st, following its inception at the UN General Assembly in 2014.

highlights: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' Economic Roadmap for Developed Nation Status, targeting sustainable and inclusive growth.
Sculptor Ram V. Sutar, creator of the Statue of Unity, celebrates his 100th birthday, marking a century of artistic contributions.
Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Affirmative Action Framework in Promotions for SCs/STs, bringing clarity to reservation policies.
India and Japan Conclude High-Level Strategic Dialogue, focusing on Indo-Pacific security, defense manufacturing, and green energy cooperation.
ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology, moving closer to cost-effective space access.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Vision Bharat 2047' economic roadmap, consider the following statements:
1. It aims to transform India into a developed nation by 2047.
2. The roadmap focuses on five pillars, including technological innovation and global economic integration.
3. A dedicated task force overseen by the Ministry of Finance will implement the vision.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The roadmap aims to transform India into a developed nation by 2047, its centenary of independence. Statement 2 is correct: The roadmap focuses on five pillars: sustainable growth, technological innovation, inclusive development, global economic integration, and robust governance. Statement 3 is incorrect: A dedicated task force, comprising experts from NITI Aayog, various ministries, and private sector leaders, will oversee the implementation, not solely the Ministry of Finance.

prelimsMocks.question: Ram V. Sutar, who celebrated his 100th birthday, is most famously associated with which of the following?
options: (c) Sculpture of the Statue of Unity
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Sculpture of the Statue of Unity
prelimsMocks.explanation: Ram V. Sutar is the renowned sculptor who designed the Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue depicting Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the Supreme Court's ruling on affirmative action in promotions:
1. The court upheld the constitutional validity of the existing framework for SC/ST promotions in public employment.
2. It mandated that quantifiable data on 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation' must be regularly reviewed.
3. The ruling applies only to central government jobs, not state government positions.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The Supreme Court reaffirmed the constitutional validity of the existing framework for affirmative action in promotions for SCs and STs. Statement 2 is correct: The ruling mandated quantifiable data on backwardness and representation across various cadres for regular review. Statement 3 is incorrect: The ruling directs both Union and State governments to finalize and implement robust mechanisms, implying it applies to both central and state public employment.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a stated focus area of the recently concluded India-Japan Strategic Dialogue?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security, defense manufacturing, green energy transition, and critical and emerging technologies (AI, quantum computing, semiconductors). Space tourism development was not mentioned as a specific focus area.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to India's National Green Hydrogen Mission, consider the following statements:
1. The revised targets aim for 7 million metric tonnes (MMT) of green hydrogen production annually by 2030.
2. The mission includes enhanced incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers.
3. It primarily targets the decarbonization of the domestic transport sector.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: The new targets aim for 7 MMT of green hydrogen production annually, up from 5 MMT. Statement 2 is correct: The revised mission includes enhanced incentives for manufacturing electrolyzers and producing green hydrogen. Statement 3 is incorrect: While transport is a potential sector, the mission aims to decarbonize 'hard-to-abate sectors like steel, refining, and fertilizers' as primary targets, positioning India as a global leader in green hydrogen export.

mainsMocks.question: The 'Vision Bharat 2047' roadmap envisions India as a developed nation. Critically analyze the challenges and opportunities for India to achieve this status, focusing on the pillars of sustainable growth and inclusive development.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment; Inclusive growth and issues arising from it.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce Vision Bharat 2047 and its goal. Elaborate on the concepts of sustainable growth (environmental, economic aspects) and inclusive development (social equity, poverty reduction). Discuss challenges like demographic dividend utilization, environmental degradation, infrastructure gaps, and regional disparities. Highlight opportunities such as technological leapfrogging, young workforce, strong domestic market, and global partnerships. Conclude with policy imperatives.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's recent ruling on affirmative action in promotions seeks to balance equality and merit. Discuss the constitutional philosophy behind reservations in public employment and critically examine the practical implications of mandating quantifiable data for its implementation.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin by explaining the constitutional provisions (Articles 16(4), 16(4A), 16(4B)) and the rationale for reservations (social justice, historical injustice, adequate representation). Discuss the 'equality of opportunity' vs. 'equality of outcome' debate. Analyze the challenges of collecting quantifiable data on 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation' (methodology, reliability, potential for litigation). Evaluate how this impacts administrative efficiency, social cohesion, and the balance with merit in public services. Conclude with suggestions for a robust and equitable implementation.

mainsMocks.question: India's space program, exemplified by ISRO's RLV efforts, is rapidly advancing towards cost-effective access to space. Evaluate the strategic and economic significance of reusable launch vehicle technology for India and its potential impact on the global space market.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS3: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology; Awareness in the fields of Space.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce ISRO's RLV program and its goal of cost-effective space access. Discuss the strategic significance: enhanced national security, self-reliance in space, support for defense. Analyze the economic significance: reduced launch costs making space more accessible for commercial payloads, fostering private sector participation, boosting India's share in the global space market, and potential for space tourism/mining. Compare with global trends (e.g., SpaceX). Conclude with challenges and future prospects for India's space ambitions.

quickBytes.text: The Statue of Unity, designed by Ram V. Sutar, is located in Kevadia, Gujarat.
NITI Aayog is the premier public policy 'think tank' of the Government of India.
The Indo-Pacific region is a biogeographic region of the Earth's seas, comprising the tropical waters of the Indian Ocean and the western and central Pacific Ocean.
The repo rate is the rate at which the Reserve Bank of India lends money to commercial banks.
The Ramsar Convention provides a framework for the conservation and wise use of wetlands and their resources.
Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) holds producers responsible for the entire lifecycle of their products, especially for post-consumer waste.
The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) was signed between India and Pakistan in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
The G20 comprises 19 countries and the European Union, representing the world's major developed and emerging economies.
The C2 cost in agricultural economics refers to comprehensive cost, including imputed value of family labour, rent on owned land, and interest on owned capital.
The Western Ghats are one of the world's eight 'hottest hotspots' of biological diversity.

GS1.headline: Sculptor Ram V. Sutar Celebrates 100th Birthday, Legacy of Iconic Statues
GS1.explainer:
• Renowned Indian sculptor Ram V. Sutar, the creative force behind the Statue of Unity, celebrates his 100th birthday today, marking a century of artistic excellence and contributions to public art.
• Sutar is best known for designing the Statue of Unity, the world's tallest statue, depicting Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel, which stands 182 meters tall in Gujarat.
• His extensive body of work includes hundreds of statues of national leaders, freedom fighters, and cultural icons, installed across India and abroad.
• A recipient of the Padma Bhushan and Padma Shri, Sutar's artistic style is characterized by a blend of realism and monumentality, capturing the essence of his subjects.
• His contributions have significantly shaped India's public spaces and national iconography, inspiring a sense of pride and historical remembrance.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
prelimsPoints: Ram V. Sutar is the sculptor of the Statue of Unity.
The Statue of Unity depicts Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.
He is a recipient of Padma Bhushan and Padma Shri.
His 100th birthday is celebrated on December 18, 2025.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of monumental public art in shaping national identity and historical consciousness in India, with examples like the Statue of Unity.
Analyze the artistic style and contributions of Ram V. Sutar to modern Indian sculpture.
Evaluate the cultural and socio-political significance of commissioning large-scale statues of national leaders.
GS1.imageDescription: Image of Ram V. Sutar alongside a miniature model of the Statue of Unity.
GS1.trivia: The Statue of Unity is located on an island in the Narmada River, facing the Sardar Sarovar Dam.

GS1.headline: UNESCO Designates Two New Indian Sites as World Heritage, Boosting Cultural Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• UNESCO today announced the inclusion of two significant Indian sites – the 'Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura' and the 'Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh' – in its prestigious World Heritage List.
• The Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura, known for their intricate sculptures and Buddhist heritage, represent a unique blend of art and spiritual traditions in Northeast India.
• The Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh, a series of strategic fortifications along major rivers, exemplify medieval Indian military architecture and urban planning.
• These designations are expected to significantly boost cultural tourism to these regions, promoting local economies and encouraging conservation efforts.
• India now boasts a total of 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites, solidifying its position as a global cultural heritage destination.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Two new Indian sites added to UNESCO World Heritage List: Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura and Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh.
India now has 44 UNESCO World Heritage Sites.
These sites represent Buddhist heritage, art, and medieval military architecture.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of UNESCO World Heritage status for the conservation, promotion, and economic development of cultural sites in India.
Analyze the unique historical and architectural importance of the 'Ancient Rock-Cut Caves of Tripura' and the 'Historic Riverine Forts of Uttar Pradesh'.
Evaluate the challenges and opportunities in managing and preserving such diverse heritage sites while promoting sustainable tourism.
GS1.imageDescription: Composite image showing a rock-cut cave entrance and a historic riverine fort.
GS1.trivia: The first Indian sites to be inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 1983 were the Ajanta Caves, Ellora Caves, Agra Fort, and the Taj Mahal.

GS1.headline: Ministry of Culture Launches 'Setu-Bharat' Cultural Exchange Program with Southeast Asian Nations
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of Culture today inaugurated 'Setu-Bharat', a new cultural exchange program aimed at strengthening ancient civilizational ties and contemporary artistic collaborations with Southeast Asian nations.
• The initiative will facilitate exchanges of artists, scholars, and cultural practitioners, promoting shared heritage in performing arts, literature, and traditional crafts.
• Key activities include joint archaeological research, heritage conservation projects, and festivals showcasing the diverse cultures of India and ASEAN member states.
• 'Setu-Bharat' seeks to enhance India's soft power diplomacy and foster greater understanding and goodwill in the region.
• The program aligns with India's 'Act East Policy' and emphasizes the historical maritime and cultural links that bind India with Southeast Asia.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Salient features of Indian Society, Diversity of India.
prelimsPoints: Ministry of Culture launched 'Setu-Bharat' cultural exchange program with Southeast Asian nations.
Aims to strengthen civilizational ties and artistic collaborations.
Includes joint archaeological research, heritage conservation, and cultural festivals.
Aligns with India's 'Act East Policy'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the role of cultural diplomacy and soft power in strengthening India's bilateral and regional relations, particularly with Southeast Asian nations.
Analyze how programs like 'Setu-Bharat' leverage shared historical and cultural heritage to foster greater understanding and cooperation.
Evaluate the significance of India's 'Act East Policy' in promoting cultural and economic engagement with the ASEAN region.
GS1.imageDescription: Collage of traditional Indian and Southeast Asian cultural symbols and art forms.
GS1.trivia: The term 'Act East Policy' succeeded the 'Look East Policy' in 2014, with a greater focus on strategic and economic engagement.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds Constitutional Validity of Affirmative Action Framework in Promotions
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court today, in a landmark judgment, reaffirmed the constitutional validity of the existing framework for affirmative action in promotions for Scheduled Castes (SCs) and Scheduled Tribes (STs) in public employment.
• The court emphasized that the state has a compelling interest in ensuring adequate representation for marginalized communities, provided the criteria for 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation' are regularly reviewed.
• The ruling clarified the parameters for data collection required to justify such reservations, mandating quantifiable data on backwardness and representation across various cadres.
• This decision brings clarity to a long-standing legal debate, aiming to balance the principles of equality and merit while addressing historical injustices.
• It directs the Union and State governments to finalize and implement robust mechanisms for periodic review and data collection within six months.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure.
Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: Supreme Court upheld affirmative action in promotions for SC/STs.
Emphasized regular review of 'backwardness' and 'inadequacy of representation'.
Mandated quantifiable data collection for justification.
mainsPoints: Analyze the constitutional principles and socio-economic justifications behind affirmative action in public employment in India.
Discuss the challenges in implementing reservations in promotions, particularly concerning the collection of quantifiable data and the balance with merit.
Evaluate the implications of the Supreme Court's ruling on administrative efficiency and social justice for marginalized communities.
GS2.imageDescription: Image of the Supreme Court of India building.
GS2.trivia: Article 16(4A) and 16(4B) of the Indian Constitution deal with reservations in promotions and carrying forward unfilled vacancies.

GS2.headline: India and Japan Conclude High-Level Strategic Dialogue, Focus on Indo-Pacific Security and Green Energy
GS2.explainer:
• India and Japan successfully concluded their annual Strategic Dialogue, emphasizing enhanced cooperation in Indo-Pacific security, defense manufacturing, and green energy transition.
• The two nations reiterated their commitment to a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific, agreeing on joint maritime exercises and intelligence sharing to counter regional challenges.
• A new framework for technology transfer and co-production in defense was announced, aiming to bolster India's 'Make in India' initiative and Japan's defense capabilities.
• Significant agreements were signed to accelerate investment in India's green hydrogen and renewable energy sectors, aligning with global climate goals.
• Both sides also discussed expanding collaboration in critical and emerging technologies, including AI, quantum computing, and semiconductors.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests, Indian diaspora.
prelimsPoints: India-Japan Strategic Dialogue focused on Indo-Pacific security and green energy.
Agreements on defense technology transfer and co-production.
Collaboration in green hydrogen, renewable energy, AI, quantum computing, semiconductors.
mainsPoints: Analyze the evolving strategic convergence between India and Japan in the context of Indo-Pacific geopolitical dynamics.
Discuss the potential of India-Japan cooperation in defense manufacturing and green energy transition for both nations' strategic autonomy and economic growth.
Evaluate how this partnership contributes to regional stability and addresses challenges posed by emerging technologies.
GS2.imageDescription: Flags of India and Japan displayed during a diplomatic meeting.
GS2.trivia: India and Japan established diplomatic relations in 1952.

GS2.headline: National Digital Health Mission Launches Unified Health Information Platform for Citizens
GS2.explainer:
• The National Digital Health Mission (NDHM) today launched its Unified Health Information Platform (UHIP), integrating various digital health services and records for citizens across the country.
• UHIP enables individuals to create unique health IDs, access their medical records securely, and connect with healthcare providers and facilities through a single portal.
• The platform aims to streamline healthcare delivery, improve patient care coordination, and facilitate data-driven policy-making in the public health sector.
• Key features include telemedicine integration, e-pharmacy services, and a consent-based framework for sharing health data with authorized entities.
• This initiative is a significant step towards achieving universal health coverage and building a resilient, accessible, and efficient digital health ecosystem in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation.
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
prelimsPoints: NDHM launched Unified Health Information Platform (UHIP).
UHIP integrates digital health services and records.
Enables unique health IDs, secure medical record access, telemedicine, and e-pharmacy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of digital health platforms like UHIP in improving healthcare accessibility and efficiency in India.
Analyze the challenges related to data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy in the widespread adoption of such platforms.
Evaluate how the National Digital Health Mission contributes to achieving the goals of universal health coverage and a resilient health system.
GS2.imageDescription: Illustration of a person accessing digital health records on a mobile device.
GS2.trivia: India's Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) is the foundational framework for the NDHM.

GS2.headline: India Initiates Further Review of Indus Waters Treaty Provisions with Pakistan
GS2.explainer:
• India has initiated a further review of certain provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) with Pakistan, citing concerns over Pakistan's interpretation and implementation of the treaty's clauses.
• The review focuses on aspects related to water usage for run-of-the-river hydropower projects on the Western rivers allocated to Pakistan, which India asserts are within its rights.
• This development follows ongoing disagreements and calls for a more equitable sharing of waters, particularly concerning projects like Kishanganga and Ratle.
• India has consistently emphasized its commitment to the IWT but also its right to utilize its share of waters for domestic, agricultural, and power generation purposes.
• Diplomatic channels remain open, but India's move signals a firmer stance on protecting its water interests in the region.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations.
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
prelimsPoints: India initiated further review of Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) provisions with Pakistan.
Concerns over Pakistan's interpretation of water usage for run-of-the-river hydropower projects on Western rivers.
IWT was signed in 1960, brokered by the World Bank.
mainsPoints: Discuss the historical context and key provisions of the Indus Waters Treaty and its significance for India-Pakistan relations.
Analyze the complexities and challenges in implementing the IWT, particularly concerning India's right to utilize Western river waters for hydropower.
Evaluate the geopolitical implications of India's review of the treaty and potential pathways for resolving water disputes between the two nations.
GS2.imageDescription: Map showing the Indus River System and the allocation of rivers under the IWT.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Waters Treaty is considered one of the most successful water-sharing agreements in the world, surviving multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers Endorse New Framework for Climate Finance Mobilization
GS2.explainer:
• G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors, meeting in Riyadh, today endorsed a new framework aimed at accelerating the mobilization of climate finance for developing nations.
• The framework emphasizes innovative financial instruments, blended finance solutions, and increased participation from multilateral development banks and the private sector.
• It also calls for greater transparency in climate finance tracking and reporting, along with capacity building for developing countries to access and utilize funds effectively.
• India played a crucial role in advocating for 'common but differentiated responsibilities and respective capabilities' (CBDR-RC) in climate finance discussions.
• The agreement is expected to pave the way for more robust financial flows towards climate adaptation, mitigation, and resilience projects globally.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests.
Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate.
keyTerms: CBDR-RC (Common But Differentiated Responsibilities and Respective Capabilities)
prelimsPoints: G20 Finance Ministers endorsed a new framework for climate finance mobilization.
Emphasizes innovative financial instruments and blended finance.
India advocated for CBDR-RC principles.
Aims to increase financial flows for climate adaptation and mitigation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the critical role of climate finance in enabling developing nations to achieve their climate goals and the challenges in its mobilization.
Analyze the key features of the new G20 framework for climate finance and its potential impact on global climate action.
Evaluate India's position and contributions to international climate finance discussions, particularly concerning equity and historical responsibility.
GS2.imageDescription: G20 logo prominently displayed at a conference venue.
GS2.trivia: The G20 was formed in 1999 in response to the Asian financial crisis.

GS2.headline: Finance Commission Recommends Enhanced Gender Budgeting Framework for State Governments
GS2.explainer:
• The 16th Finance Commission, in its interim report submitted today, recommended an enhanced framework for gender budgeting to be adopted by all state governments across India.
• The recommendations include mandatory allocation of a specific percentage of state budgets towards schemes directly benefiting women and girls, with robust monitoring mechanisms.
• The framework emphasizes disaggregated data collection on gender-sensitive indicators to assess the impact and effectiveness of public expenditure.
• It also suggests capacity building for state finance departments and local bodies to integrate a gender perspective into planning and resource allocation.
• This move aims to promote gender equality, empower women, and ensure that public spending effectively addresses gender disparities in development outcomes.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: 16th Finance Commission recommended enhanced gender budgeting framework for state governments.
Mandates specific budget allocation for schemes benefiting women and girls.
Emphasizes disaggregated data collection and capacity building.
Aims to promote gender equality and address disparities.
mainsPoints: Discuss the concept and significance of gender budgeting in promoting gender equality and inclusive development in India.
Analyze the recommendations of the 16th Finance Commission for state governments regarding gender budgeting and their potential impact on public finance and women's empowerment.
Evaluate the challenges in effective implementation of gender budgeting, including data availability, political will, and capacity constraints at the state level.
GS2.imageDescription: Graphic illustrating the concept of gender equality with balanced scales.
GS2.trivia: India introduced gender budgeting statements in its Union Budget for the first time in 2005-06.

GS2.headline: AYUSH Ministry Launches Advanced Yoga Protocol for Mental Well-being and Stress Management
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of AYUSH today unveiled an advanced yoga protocol specifically designed to enhance mental well-being, manage stress, and mitigate anxiety in daily life.
• Developed in collaboration with leading yoga institutions and mental health experts, the protocol integrates specific asanas, pranayama, meditation techniques, and mindfulness practices.
• The initiative targets a broad demographic, including students, working professionals, and the elderly, providing accessible tools for mental health support.
• Training modules and digital resources will be made available to promote widespread adoption and ensure correct practice of the protocol.
• This launch underscores the government's commitment to promoting holistic health and leveraging traditional Indian practices for contemporary health challenges.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources.

Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States and the performance of these schemes; mechanisms, laws, institutions and Bodies constituted for the protection and betterment of these vulnerable sections.
prelimsPoints: AYUSH Ministry launched an advanced yoga protocol for mental well-being and stress management.
Integrates asanas, pranayama, meditation, and mindfulness.
Targets students, professionals, and elderly.
Promotes holistic health using traditional Indian practices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing challenge of mental health issues in contemporary society and the role of traditional practices like yoga in addressing them.
Analyze the components and potential benefits of the AYUSH Ministry's advanced yoga protocol for mental well-being and stress management.
Evaluate the government's efforts in promoting holistic health and integrating traditional medicine systems into mainstream healthcare for public welfare.
GS2.imageDescription: People performing yoga postures in a serene outdoor setting.
GS2.trivia: The International Day of Yoga is celebrated annually on June 21st, following its inception at the UN General Assembly in 2014.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils 'Vision Bharat 2047' Economic Roadmap for Developed Nation Status
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government today launched the ambitious 'Vision Bharat 2047' economic roadmap, outlining a comprehensive strategy to transform India into a developed nation by its centenary of independence.
• The roadmap focuses on five pillars: sustainable growth, technological innovation, inclusive development, global economic integration, and robust governance.
• Key initiatives include massive infrastructure investment, skill development programs for the future workforce, promotion of green technologies, and reforms to boost manufacturing and exports.
• A dedicated task force, comprising experts from NITI Aayog, various ministries, and private sector leaders, will oversee the implementation and progress of the vision.
• The plan aims to significantly increase per capita income, reduce poverty, and improve India's ranking on global human development indices.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Vision Bharat 2047 aims for developed nation status by 2047.
Five pillars: sustainable growth, technological innovation, inclusive development, global economic integration, robust governance.
Implementation overseen by a task force with NITI Aayog involvement.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of a long-term economic roadmap like Vision Bharat 2047 for India's developmental trajectory.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in achieving the 'developed nation' goal by 2047, considering the five identified pillars.
Evaluate the potential impact of proposed reforms on various sectors of the Indian economy and society.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic representing India's economic growth trajectory towards 2047.
GS3.trivia: India's first five-year plan was launched in 1951, focusing on agricultural development.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) Technology
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) today announced the successful completion of a critical test for its next-generation Reusable Launch Vehicle (RLV) technology, marking a major step towards cost-effective space access.
• The test involved a scaled-down prototype demonstrating autonomous landing capabilities under simulated operational conditions, validating key aerodynamic and guidance systems.
• This achievement is crucial for ISRO's long-term goal of developing a fully reusable two-stage orbital launch vehicle, significantly reducing the cost per launch.
• The RLV program aims to enhance India's competitive edge in the global space market, allowing for frequent and affordable access to space for satellites and future human spaceflight missions.
• Further tests are planned, including orbital re-entry and hypersonic flight demonstrations, as ISRO pushes the boundaries of space technology.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life.
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
